_meta.notes: Floor + residual identity from phaseB; six gate verdicts with numbers pulled from each gate CSV.

floor.label: cross-donor per-perturbation specific signal (top-50 DEG)
floor.triangulation: the same ~0.03 floor recurs in the budget cross-donor test, the SNR pre-check, the trajectory scalar, the donor Δ, and the relational objects.

gates.name: Causal-matrix (Â_C)
gates.headline: Explicit per-context causal matrix does not beat correlation under P≪G.
gates.aside: Standalone positive: the linear transportability condition itself degrades (oracle AUROC 1.00 → 0.88) as the system becomes nonlinear.

gates.name: Fluctuation (third moment)
gates.headline: On real data the baseline third moment is orthogonal to the residual — the simulator signal does not survive.

key_number.label: ΔR² across strata
key_number.detail: mean 0.0000 over 24/24 strata (was +0.6–0.75 on the simulator)

gates.aside: The covariance/Lyapunov sufficiency that makes correlation hard to beat is a second-moment property; the third moment was the one place signal could hide. It does not, on CD4 data.

gates.name: Single-cell resolution (CellCap SNR)
gates.headline: Single-cell resolution would not recover the per-perturbation residual — a confirmed noise floor at pseudobulk depth.

key_number.label: fraction detectable at SNR≥3
key_number.detail: reaching a usable floor needs ~12× more cells; green = 0

gates.aside: Reached with zero GPU / zero egress — no 130 GB of single cells were pulled to prove a negative.

gates.name: Trajectory-coupling
gates.headline: Recoverability is not a trajectory-geometry property — no dynamical model was fit or needed.
gates.aside: The reduced scalar target itself sits at the noise floor (~0.07 vs random-axis null 0.03).

gates.name: Donor-structured recovery
gates.headline: Within-donor same-gene structure is real but at noise-floor magnitude, and donor-averaging beats donor-conditioning — the reversal is refuted.

key_number.label: same-gene within-donor Δ
key_number.detail: 4/4 donors, well below the Δ≥0.15 bar; conditioning 0.016 < averaging 0.034 (the gap inverts)

gates.aside: An earlier within-donor reproducibility figure that motivated this test was a noise-model estimate, not an empirical measurement — and the real gap inverts.

gates.name: Relational (perturbation similarity)
gates.headline: Cross-donor relational structure (which perturbations resemble which) is at the floor — all five relational objects fail.

key_number.label: cross-donor S-similarity (specific)
key_number.detail: the strong cross-donor similarity a response-theory simulator suggested does not appear — measured 0.008, at the floor. 5/5 objects fail.

gates.aside: The last of the six, spanning to relational + raw structure — the floor holds even when you ask which perturbations look alike.

summary: Six pre-registered negatives — causal-matrix, fluctuation, single-cell, trajectory, donor-structure, relational — converge on the same pointwise floor, spanning pointwise and relational, raw and specific. Zero GPU across all six. We bounded the frontier honestly; we did not solve perturbation prediction.
